experiments.name: HVAC owner discovery and paid pilot test
experiments.summary: Ran 12 HVAC owner conversations to test missed-call pain and willingness to pay for a pilot.
outcomes: 4 of 12 said missed calls are painful
1 churned after setup because the workflow was too custom

strong_signals: 1 customer actually paid, showing real willingness to buy
4 HVAC owners repeated missed calls as a painful problem
2 owners agreed to a paid pilot, indicating stronger-than-verbal interest
weak_signals: Only 4 of 12 conversations surfaced the target pain clearly, so the wedge is not yet broad across all HVAC owners
contradictions: A paying pilot still churned after setup because the workflow was too custom, showing retention and delivery risk despite demand
next_experiment: Run a standardized paid pilot with 3 HVAC owners who all report missed-call pain, using the same onboarding and workflow with minimal customization, then measure activation and retention through the initial pilot period.
rationale: There is real demand signal because owners expressed repeated pain and one customer paid, but the churn outcome shows the current solution may be too bespoke to scale. The next experiment should test whether a tighter segment and a standardized workflow can retain customers without custom setup.
